name: Kerndt Brothers Savings Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank in Iowa offering checking, savings, CDs, mortgages, agricultural loans, and business lending since 1856.
description_long:
Kerndt Brothers Savings Bank is an established Iowa-based community bank with over 165 years of history, operating since 1856. The bank maintains multiple branch locations across Iowa (Cedar Rapids, Decorah, Waukon, West Union) and is fully FDIC-insured, backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of retail banking products including personal and business checking accounts, savings accounts, money market accounts, CDs, IRAs, and HSAs. They provide consumer lending through personal loans, home mortgage loans, home equity loans, auto loans, and solar program loans. On the business side, Kerndt Brothers offers business checking, business savings, business loans, commercial real estate loans, equipment loans, SBA loans, and business lines of credit. Agricultural customers have access to specialized ag loans including land loans, livestock loans, and operating capital loans.

Kerndt Brothers distinguishes itself through the Kasasa product suite, which includes free checking accounts with rewards (Kasasa Cash Checking and Kasasa Cash Back Checking) and high-yield savings accounts. They emphasize digital banking capabilities including mobile banking, online banking, and surcharge-free ATM access. The bank also offers insurance products (personal, business, farm/crop, and life insurance) and investment services. Their heritage travel club provides customer benefits beyond traditional banking.

As a regional community bank, Kerndt Brothers is best suited for customers in their service areas who value local relationship banking and agricultural lending expertise. However, they may have higher fees or lower rates compared to larger national banks or online competitors. Their specific product rates and terms require direct contact, and service availability varies by branch location. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal checking accounts (Kasasa Cash, Kasasa Cash Back, EZ Checking, Basic Checking, Interest Checking)
Personal savings accounts (Kasasa Saver, Regular Savings, Companion Savings, Christmas Club, Money Market)
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with promotional rates
Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) and Health Savings Accounts (HSAs)
Home mortgage loans and home equity loans
Agricultural loans (land loans, livestock loans, equipment loans, operating capital)
Business checking and business savings accounts
Business loans, commercial real estate loans, and SBA loans
Digital banking, mobile banking, and online account management
Insurance services (personal, business, farm/crop, life insurance)
Investment services and financial planning tools
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government guarantee
Free checking accounts with cash rewards (Kasasa Cash and Kasasa Cash Back checking)
Specialized agricultural lending including livestock loans, land loans, and operating capital loans
Comprehensive business banking suite including SBA loans and commercial real estate lending
High-yield savings options (advertised 4.05% APY on 9-month CDs with Companion Savings)
Digital banking platform with mobile banking and surcharge-free ATM network
Full-service insurance and investment services in-house
Long-established local presence in Iowa since 1856 with multiple branch locations
cons: Limited geographic service area (Iowa only) — not available for customers outside their branch footprint
Smaller institution may offer fewer products or less competitive rates than large national banks
Specific APY rates, fees, and loan terms not disclosed on website — requires direct inquiry
May have higher minimum balance requirements or monthly fees on basic accounts compared to online banks
Limited information about mobile app features, customer service hours, or response times
best_for: Iowa farmers and agricultural business owners seeking specialized ag lending and local relationship banking
Small business owners in Iowa seeking comprehensive business lending, checking, and cash management services
Consumers in Cedar Rapids, Decorah, Waukon, or West Union seeking FDIC-insured banking with local branch access
Customers interested in reward-checking accounts and who value community bank relationship banking
diagnosis: Kerndt Brothers Savings Bank is best for Iowa-based consumers and businesses seeking full-service community banking with strong agricultural lending expertise and FDIC-insured products. Primary caveat: service is geographically limited to Iowa locations, and customers outside their branch areas will not have access to their products or services.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
